issue_title: pylint throws a RecursionError for toml.py
记录域名301过程中跳转的所有域名
dRep failes when no genome passes the filtering step
issue_body:
Hi, as recommended in #25 I ran dRep with this option but it seems this is not implemented correctly in the current version:

```
***************************************************
    ..:: dRep dereplicate Step 2. Cluster ::..
***************************************************
    
Clustering Step 1. Parse Arguments
Clustering Step 2. Perform MASH (primary) clustering
2. Nevermind! Skipping Mash
0 primary clusters made
Step 3. Perform secondary clustering
Running 0 ANImf comparisons- should take ~ 0.0 min
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/bin/dRep", line 26, in <module>
    controller.parseArguments(args)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/drep/controller.py", line 144, in parseArguments
    self.dereplicate_operation(**vars(args))
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/drep/controller.py", line 86, in dereplicate_operation
    drep.d_workflows.dereplicate_wrapper(kwargs['work_directory'],**kwargs)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/drep/d_workflows.py", line 36, in dereplicate_wrapper
    drep.d_cluster.d_cluster_wrapper(wd, **kwargs)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/drep/d_cluster.py", line 75, in d_cluster_wrapper
    data_folder, wd=workDirectory, **kwargs)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/drep/d_cluster.py", line 175, in cluster_genomes
    Cdb, c2ret = _cluster_Ndb(Ndb, comp_method=algorithm, **kwargs)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/drep/d_cluster.py", line 207, in _cluster_Ndb
    for name, ndb in Ndb.groupby(id):
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas/core/generic.py", line 5162, in groupby
    **kwargs)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas/core/groupby.py", line 1848, in groupby
    return klass(obj, by, **kwds)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas/core/groupby.py", line 516, in __init__
    mutated=self.mutated)
  File "/home/johdro/.conda/envs/drep/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pandas/core/groupby.py", line 2934, in _get_grouper
    raise KeyError(gpr)
KeyError: 'primary_cluster'
```

Apparently, skipping MASH produces zero primary clusters and this leads to errors.

Command line used was

    dRep dereplicate -pa 0.8 -sa 0.98 -comp 25 -con 25 -l 20000 -p 1 --SkipMash drep_out -g *.fna

Best,
Johannes

issue_title: setup.cfg contains a version, causing pip install directly from github to fail
issue_body:
When I use pip to install the `retry` version from Github, I get the following error:
```
  Updating ./src/decorator clone
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    ERROR:root:Error parsing
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/core.py", line 111, in pbr
        attrs = util.cfg_to_args(path, dist.script_args)
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/util.py", line 246, in cfg_to_args
        pbr.hooks.setup_hook(config)
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/hooks/__init__.py", line 25, in setup_hook
        metadata_config.run()
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/hooks/base.py", line 27, in run
        self.hook()
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/hooks/metadata.py", line 26, in hook
        self.config['name'], self.config.get('version', None))
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/packaging.py", line 701, in get_version
        version = _get_version_from_git(pre_version)
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/packaging.py", line 641, in _get_version_from_git
        result = _get_version_from_git_target(git_dir, target_version)
      File "/tmp/src/decorator/.eggs/pbr-1.9.1-py2.6.egg/pbr/packaging.py", line 603, in _get_version_from_git_target
        dict(new=new_version, target=target_version))
    ValueError: git history requires a target version of pbr.version.SemanticVersion(0.9.2), but target version is pbr.version.SemanticVersion(0.9.1)
    error in setup command: Error parsing /tmp/src/decorator/setup.cfg: ValueError: git history requires a target version of pbr.version.SemanticVersion(0.9.2), but target version is pbr.version.SemanticVersion(0.9.1)
```

This happens because setup.cfg mentions an explicit version number, but the current commit is ahead of the last version tag.

Could you remove this version specifier (like it was done [here](https://bugs.launchpad.net/python-manilaclient/+bug/1395067)) or release a new version soon? I'm currently stuck in dependency limbo because of the `decorator`<4.0.0 limitation in 0.9.1.

issue_title: What the heck is the "Manifold" object?
issue_body:
I've been going through the source-code for this library, and trying to write code that uses it.

From what I can tell, everything seems to be piped through the "Manifold" object, which seems to be global (?), and looks a *lot* like a [reactor](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reactor_pattern).

What the hell **is** a "Manifold"? It's not documented *anywhere* in the documentation, and where it's mentioned it's treated as calling it "the manifold" is self-explanitory.

The only results for googling lead to either the Manifold GIS system, or wikipedia pages for unrelated gometric terminology.

If it's functionally a reactor (and it certainly looks kind of like one), can you possibly use standard terminology, or at least add a blurb to the documentation ("the manifold is a reactor. We're using this non-standard term for {insert reason here}")?

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/jaraco/irc/issue/48
- Originally reported by: Fake Name
- Originally created at: 2014-11-14T09:48:39.426

issue_title: Module uses select, fails to work on boxes with lots of open files
issue_body:
Trying to start a connection always gives:


```
#!python
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/irc/client.py", line 278, in process_forever
    self.process_once(timeout)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/irc/client.py", line 258, in process_once
    (i, o, e) = select.select(sockets, [], [], timeout)
ValueError: filedescriptor out of range in select()

```

Because the fileno generated by the socket can vary from 1250 to 6919. Module should use poll or epoll if available

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/jaraco/irc/issue/65
- Originally reported by: Anonymous
- Originally created at: 2015-09-21T20:34:59.729

issue_title: Antenna selection Limesdr not saving
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
Selecting the antenna  from SA or Sniff should save when you go to record signal.

## Actual Behavior
Antenna selection for LimeSDR is not saving


## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

  1. Open URH
  2. Select SA and set parameters - Device-LimeSDR / Channel-RX1 Antenna-Wide RX_W / Frequency / Gain
  3. Close SA and go to Record Signal - All parameters are saved except for Antenna - It seems to revert to RX_L

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11001973/42415789-540bd5c8-829f-11e8-9178-2736a65a951d.png)


## Platform Specifications

  - Operating System: Windows 10
  - Version of URH: 1.2.2
  - URH was installed: .msi

issue_title: Spectrum analyzer needs a lot of CPU (with rtl-tcp)
issue_body:
Hi,

Currently using the 1.6.1.8 version of URH

Starting spectrum analyzer with RTL-TCP + 900 kSps consume a lot of CPU.
My 8-core Intel i7 3820QM 2.7 GHz gets 7 cores flooded à 100%

Consecutively, I see a low pretty refresh rate : a pulse sent every 30s isn't seen after 2 minutes of waiting

I know signal processing needs a lot of resources, but is this case normal ?

issue_title: 0.8 is coming in April 15, and 0.9 will be faster due to new committers
issue_body:
Just a quick update. It has been a year since the last release and it is definitely time for new one. The largest change is the refactoring of the registry (which I have been using for a while and works nicely). But there a lot of other very valuable features and bug fixes already merged.

I would also like to mention that we are now using https://github.com/bors-ng/bors-ng to improve the PR approval workflow. This will be supported by giving members of the community committers rights.

Thanks!

issue_title: isbn10 and isbn13 do not work when downloaded from pypi.python.org
issue_body:
Hello,

the methods isbn10() and isbn13() raise an AttributeError when called from a virtualenv. It's because the faker/providers/isbn/ directory is not embedded in the library. When the code is executed from a cloned repository, it works because the directory exists.
I guess that setuptools.find_packages() does not find it so it's not included when the library is built.



Examples:
When the code is executed from a cloned repository, it works properly:
$ ./venv/bin/python
Python 3.4.3rc1 (default, Mar 17 2015, 16:22:37) 
[GCC 4.6.3] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from faker import Factory
fake = Factory.create()
>>> fake.isbn10()
'1-4325-7338-1'
>>> fake.isbn13()
'978-0-424-29655-5'

However it does not work when the library is installed in a virtualenv from pypi.python.org:
$ ./venv/bin/python
Python 3.4.3rc1 (default, Mar 17 2015, 16:22:37) 
[GCC 4.6.3] on linux
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from faker import Factory
>>> fake = Factory.create()
>>> fake.isbn10()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: 'Generator' object has no attribute 'isbn10'
>>> fake.isbn13()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: 'Generator' object has no attribute 'isbn13'

issue_title: Refactor Profile to be used with locale: how
issue_body:
I got this idea but i'm not sure it would be the simplest: the actual profile.py becomes something like "internal_profile.py", its methods are renamed "internal_simple_profile()" and "internal_profile()", and is removed from the list of standard providers. Then we will have a standard profile.py that simply calls self.generator.internal_profile(). For each locale instead, we will be able to add more logic, for example to customize field names and eventually values. 

Do you think there would be a simpler way to do it?

issue_title: past_datetime() crashes when start_date is within one second of now
issue_body:
I ran into a problem while generating a bunch of fake dates where I was calling `fake.past_datetime()` with the start date using the result of a previous `fake.past_datetime()` call (simulating a bunch of files being created as part of a collection). The first call could randomly return a value which is within one second of the current time, causing the second call to trigger an exception from `random.randint()` because the `a` and `b` values were the same.

This was pretty easy to solve simply by using `fake.date_time_between(start_date=previous_created_date)` but it might be useful if `past_datetime()` was safe to call like this.

issue_title: S3 Event Source put-bucket-notification-configuration call overwrites any pre-existing notification configuration
issue_body: The S3 event source add function sends a new "LambdaFunctionConfigurations" array in the json block on it's "put_bucket_notification_configuration". This overwrites any existing elements that may already be in this array in the existing configuration. The method should check first to see if there are already elements, and then add a new element.

issue_title: Windows: referencing .pdf figures from temp directory
issue_body:
I was following along with one of the examples in the [docs](https://jeltef.github.io/PyLaTeX/current/examples/matplotlib_ex.html) and noticed an issue when trying to make my final .pdf report. 

Explicitly I was receiving this warning which resulted in the figure not showing up in my final report.
```LaTeX Warning: File `c:/users/acomer\nobreakspace {}1/appdata/local/temp/pylatex/37811b40-d23b-47a5-b77d-3199b50f36b5.pdf' not found on input line 17.```

Line 17 of generated .tex:
```\includegraphics[width=1\textwidth]{{c:/users/acomer~1/appdata/local/temp/pylatex/37811b40-d23b-47a5-b77d-3199b50f36b5}.pdf}```

I think this is because ```~``` is interpreted as ```r\nobreakspace {}``` in the ```.tex``` file. ```acomerford``` is my username and replacing that section with my username enabled me to see the figure I was trying to show. 

I think it's an easy fix to go into ```pylatex/figure.py``` and include in either ```add_image```(Line 20) or maybe ```StandAloneGraphic```(Line 136) and include the path in double quotes or something

issue_title: DBusException on import (AccessDenied and NoReply) on certain environment
issue_body:
The issue was originally reported for packaging of isrcsubmit in JonnyJD/isrcsubmit#108.

The problem is that `import keyring` fails when run by a user with an environment "inherited" by another user. The user does not have access to the dbus session of the original user and that breaks keyring already at the import.

The "inherited" environment is wrong, but keyring should not care about that at `import`.

The version of keyring I am running is 6.1.1 and python-secretstorage 2.1.3.

An example failing environmet (when running `import keyring` with an id of 1007):
```
DISPLAY=:0.1
DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1000/bus
```

The error messages were different for 2 different users. For one user (@Freso) only `DISPLAY` was relevant and the error message on import was:
`dbus.exceptions.DBusException: org.freedesktop.DBus.Error.NoReply: Did not receive a reply. Possible causes include: the remote application did not send a reply, the message bus security policy blocked the reply, the reply timeout expired, or the network connection was broken.`

For my the dbus session variable was relevant and I got this error:
`dbus.exceptions.DBusException: org.freedesktop.DBus.Error.AccessDenied: Failed to connect to socket /run/user/1000/bus: Permission denied`. I do have to unset `DISPLAY`, too. Otherwise the import takes several seconds.

issue_title: Possible interface improvements
issue_body:
Hi! I recently discovered your package and I think it is great. I have a few suggestions about the API. Consider this example:
```
d_dx = FinDiff(h=[dx, dy, dz, du], dims=[0])
df_dx = d_dx(f)
```
Do you really need `[dy, dz, du]` if `dims` is equal to `[0]`? Consider the following interface
```
diff_result = FinDiff(f, (dim1, delta1, count1), (dim2, delta2, count2), ...)
```
where the last `count` fields are optional with a default value of `1`. For example,
```
df_dx = FinDiff(f, (0, dx))
df_dx2 = FinDiff(f, (0, dx, 2))
df_dxdy = FinDiff(f, (0, dx), (1, dy))
```
Also, if one needs a callable, it is always easy to use a `lambda`:
```
d_dx = lambda x: FinDiff(x, (0, dx))
```
Finally, to support non-uniform grids, you can check whether the `delta` field is a scalar or a vector.

I have a few other suggestions, I'm curious to hear what you think of these first. Again, thanks for publishing this package. I think it is weird that NumPy doesn't have this built-in, and this package fills an important gap. Maybe it will eventually find its way into NumPy.

issue_title: [Question] zip_safe setting if Wheels
issue_body:
### 1. Briefly

I'm not sure, is the parameter `zip_safe` needs, if developer build package use [**Wheels**](https://pythonwheels.com/).

### 2. Steps to reproduce

I create my Python package → [**I build my package, use Wheels**](https://packaging.python.org/tutorials/distributing-packages/#platform-wheels):

    python setup.py bdist_wheel

I publish my package to PyPI use [**Twine**](https://github.com/pypa/twine):

    twine upload dist/*

I install my package use pip:

    pip install <my_package>

### 3. Essence of the question

If I build my package, use `python setup.py sdist`, `zip_safe` parameter have a meaning:

If `zip_safe = True`:

    package add to my `site-packages` directory, packed to `.egg` format;

else:

issue_title: Using shortcut() to read values updates lnk-file
issue_body:
Hi,

Seems that using the shortcut() function modifies existing lnk-file even if I don't modify any of its properties. Consider following code which just gets lnk-file's target path: 
```python
def get_lnk_targetpath(lnk_filepath):
	with winshell.shortcut(lnk_filepath) as link:
		return link.path

get_lnk_targetpath("path\\to\\file.lnk")
```
When I check the lnk-file on disk, its 'last modified' -field has updated which is unexpected since I didn't change anything.

As a workaround I can patch the shortcut object's write() -method to do nothing:
```python
def get_lnk_targetpath(lnk_filepath):
	with winshell.shortcut(lnk_filepath) as link:
		link.write = lambda: None
		return link.path
```

issue_title: passive scan stops when connecting to bluetooth device
issue_body:
i have 2 threads, 1 running passive scan, which should run forever, and 1 for setting up connections and executing tasks.
The moment i try to connect to the peripheral, my scanner stops.

Code to reproduce:

```python
import threading
import time

from bluepy.btle import Peripheral, Scanner

mac = "a0:e6:f8:ce:1a:02"


def do_scan():
    """ The scanning thread function """
    scanner = Scanner()
    scanner.scan(0, passive=True)
    print "Done scanning."

def do_connect():
    """ Connecting in main thread"""
    # sleep to give the scanner some startup time
    time.sleep(4)
    p = Peripheral()
    p.connect(mac)
    time.sleep(2)
    p.disconnect()

scan_thread = threading.Thread(target=do_scan)
scan_thread.setDaemon(True)
scan_thread.start()

do_connect()

```

issue_title: error while parsing /proc/net/unix (psutil 3.2.0)
Question: VM and Container CPU & Memory Stats
issue_body:
I am using the Python library PSUtil on a Ubuntu machine to gather CPU, memory, and network (Host and processes) usage stats for a web server executed directly on the host OS, in a VM (VMWare Workstation), and in a container. To get process specific stats, I pass in the process id (pid) of the software process, the hypervisor, or the container process to get stats for each execution method. I use all default settings for PSUtil and have read the PSUtil documentation extensively but I have some questions:

1) The VM and container each create their own virtual network interface. When I collect network data (for example bytes sent) on the host machine, does PSUtil only record data seen my the host's NIC or does it record the amount of data seen by all of the NICs (the host's NIC + each virtual NIC)?

2) If I run the software in a VM, PSUtil reports that the Host Memory Usage Percentage is half the percentage than if I ran the software non-virtualized (directly on the host). At the same time, the VM's Process Memory in bytes is double the size than when the software is non-virtualized. How can PSUtil report Host memory usage percentage is much lower when running a full virtual machine than non-virtualized? Is the virtualization causing PSUtil to skew/underreport memory usage?

issue_title: Integrating Bezier curves
issue_body:
In svg.path from PiP, there is a following commet:
```
# Apparently it's impossible to integrate a Cubic Bezier, so
# this is a geometric approximation instead.
```

Well, Bezier cubes are polynomials, and AFAIK polynomials are one of simplest functions to integrate (well, sin and cos may be simpler).

So unless I'm missing some crucial detail here, computing the length of a Bezier curve can be done analytically, with major speedup.

On a side note: it should be noted that Path.point() does not guarantee points being equally distant on curve when linearly changing argument is used. Wighting dispatching of Path.point to segment by segment weights helps, but Bezier curves internally don't behave that way.

issue_title: Add support for Windows xterm emulators.
issue_body:
Migrated from https://code.google.com/p/colorama/issues/detail?id=16
Reported by deldie, Jul 24, 2010

What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. Launch python.exe win32 binary under xterm, rxvt, or mintty terminal emulators for Cygwin.
2. Execute an script with colorama.init() and ANSI escape sequences.

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?
It should show colored output, but it strips the escape sequences.
I guess it should work with the Python Cygwin port as the value of sys.platform, according to the Python docs, is 'cygwin' and not 'win32'.

What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?
The last version of colorama module, Python 2.7 Win32 (not that from the Cygwin package) and Windows XP SP3.

Please provide any additional information below.
I've resolved this adding these lines to my example code:

```python
import os
from colorama import init

terminal = os.getenv('TERM')

if terminal is None or 'rxvt' not in terminal:
    init(autoreset=True)
else:
    init(autoreset=True, convert=False, strip=False)

print "\033[45m" "HOLA"
print "HOLA"
```

This example code outputs the same result either under CMD.EXE or under Cygwin default console or under rxvt terminal, only the first HOLA is magenta.

I think it would be great to support the Windows xterm emulators in colorama.

issue_title: str vs bytes issue in download
issue_body:
a1837d1007179c14fd309606b94e7d00459de7fc changed: `data += ET.tostring(tree, encoding='unicode')` to `data += ET.tostring(tree, encoding='utf-8')` but I argue this is incorrect since `data` is a `str` and we need the `tostring` to return a `str`, but with the encoding in Python 3 it will return `bytes`

Anyway before making a diff to put it back I wanted to check with @fergbrain whether he had seen an issue.

issue_title: OS X, LibreOffice 4.4 and 5.0: no suitable office installation
issue_body:
As mentioned in #211, unoconv does not detect a suitable office installation on OS X (Yosemite) and LibreOffice 4.4 and 5.0. LibreOffice 4.3 works fine. When fixing manually the path searching to ensure that everything is found, the error becomes `ImportError: No module named 'com' (or 'com.sun.star.document.UpdateDocMode.QUIET_UPDATE' is unknown)`. Amusingly, this is the only import that breaks, but other things break anyway if we leave this out.

I think this is a bug in `find_offices` and `office_environ` only, because using the `python` binary from LibreOffice 4.4 and 5.0, this code works fine:

```
import uno
import unohelper

from com.sun.star.beans import PropertyValue
from com.sun.star.connection import NoConnectException
from com.sun.star.document.UpdateDocMode import QUIET_UPDATE
from com.sun.star.lang import DisposedException, IllegalArgumentException
from com.sun.star.io import IOException, XOutputStream
from com.sun.star.script import CannotConvertException
from com.sun.star.uno import Exception as UnoException
from com.sun.star.uno import RuntimeException
```

Since #211 is closed, I'll use this issue to document my progress, if any.

issue_title: Updating a directory copies destination to wrong path
issue_body:
Hi there.
I have been using dotdrop for quite some time now and I really love it.

Since last month's commit, I have started tracking whole directories and noticed that using dotdrop to update a directory whose source's and destination's base-names are different will result in a new copy inside the dotfiles directory.

For example, let's say that I track the directory `~/foo.d` which is stored in `~/.dotfiles/bar.d` (according to my `config.yaml`). Running:
`dotdrop.sh update ~/foo.d` 
will copy that directory to `~/.dotfiles/foo.d` instead of `~/.dotfiles/bar.d`.
The reason for this seems to be the current implementation of `update` which actually executes:
`['cp', '-R', '-L', '/home/maxchaos/foo.d', '/home/maxchaos/.dotfiles/foo.d/..']`

I guess an easy fix to this issue would be passing the option `-T` to `cp` like that:
`['cp', '-R', '-L', '-T', '/home/maxchaos/foo.d', '/home/maxchaos/.dotfiles/bar.d']`

Any ideas?
Thanks in advance.

issue_title: Allow nullable values
issue_body:
I would like to add a way to validate nullable values.  I thought that the format validation might allow me to do this, but it is separate from the type validation.   I don't mind submitting the code, but I would love a pointer on where to start.  

The biggest potential issue is that swagger does not officially define nullable fields. In fact, `null` isn't allowed in the spec. So either a spec would need to be picked or it would need to be configurable. We have been using `x-nullable: true` in our specs.   For example,

```yaml
properties:
  id:
    type: integer
    format: int32
  organization_id:
    type: integer
    format: int32
  email:
    type: string
  first_name:
    type: string
    x-nullable: true
```

As per https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-spec/issues/229, `x-nullable` and `x-isnullable` seem to be popular choices for people with nullable fields.

issue_title: AttributeError: 'Request' object has no attribute 'log'
issue_body:
If there is a problem with parsing the posted JSON to DRF API endpoint, drf-tracking raises this exception and causes Internal Server Error. Reason is that inside LoggingMixin.initial if there is a ParseError thrown on data_dict = request.data.dict() self.request.log is never set, but finalize_response gets called anyway.

Problem is, you never get to see the actual problem (i.e. `{"detail":"JSON parse error - Expecting property name enclosed in double quotes: line 7 column 1 (char 111)"}`), as the 500 Internal Error masks the problem.

issue_title: "# normalize args by converting args like --key=value to --key value" breaks "--key=-value"
issue_body:
This is a discrepancy between `configargparse` and `argparse`.

Suppose I want to pass an argument to an option where the argument starts with a hyphen. This works with argparse:

```
>>> import argparse
>>> parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
>>> parser.add_argument('--stuff')
>>> args = parser.parse_args(['--stuff=--thing'])
>>> args.stuff
'--thing'
```
but the normalization step breaks this for configargparse:

```
>>> import configargparse
>>> parser = configargparse.ArgumentParser()
>>> parser.add_argument('--stuff')
>>> args = parser.parse_args(["--stuff=--thing"])
usage: [-h] [--stuff STUFF]
: error: argument --stuff: expected one argument
```

The reason this breaks is because of [Python bug #9334](https://bugs.python.org/issue9334), which will prevent arguments to an option as parsing as such when they have a leading hyphen. (Yes, this is contrary to every other argument parsing library ever written, including `getopt_long`.)

Is this normalization step required?

issue_title: Output coroutine functions
issue_body:
It would be great if `@decorator` could be used to create decorators which produce [coroutine functions](https://docs.python.org/3/library/inspect.html#inspect.iscoroutinefunction).  Is there already such functionality and I just didn't find it? If not, would this be feasible? If so, are there any plans for this? Would you accept PRs?

Example:
```python
from inspect import iscoroutinefunction
from decorator import decorator

@decorator
async def ensure_coro(function, *args, **kwargs): 
    ret = function(*args, **kwargs)
    if iscoroutinefunction(function):
        ret = await ret
    return ret

@ensure_coro
def foo():
    return 42

assert iscoroutinefunction(foo)
```

issue_title: FCMDeviceAuthorizedViewSet allows duplicate registrations with same device ID and registration token
issue_body:
I've hooked up FCMDeviceAuthorizedViewSet to Django REST framework using DefaultRouter as described in the manual, and configured my app to POST to my base URL `/api/fcm/`. Registration seems to be working nicely, except for the fact that the creation view by FCMDeviceAuthorizedViewSet seems to allow for the same device ID to be registered to the same user again, even if the registration token is identical.

The manual states "Will allow duplicate registration_id’s for different users, so you are responsible for cleanup (if that is generally perceived as undesired behaviour or if the package itself should be doing the cleanup, open an issue or email me)". It seems that the most logical behavior would be to allow one user to register multiple **unique** device IDs, and only update the registration_token for existing device IDs.

issue_title: Test Unicode and bytes handling (Python 2 and 3) in all string arguments
issue_body:
After merging #119, the `file` argument should support `str` and `unicode` in Python 2 and `str` and `bytes` in Python 3. The arguments `mode`/`format`/`subtype`/`endian` should support `str` and `unicode` in Python 2 and only `str` in Python 3 (`bytes` should be disallowed there).

There are some facts that are especially annoying when testing this:

* in Python2, `unicode` can be implicitly converted/compared to `str` (as long as the string consists of only ASCII characters), this is not possible for Python3's `str` and `bytes`. That means that test cases that pass in Python2 may fail in Python3.
* file names should be tested with both Unicode and byte strings. A `bytes` object may also contain non-ASCII characters. All combinations of Unicode/bytes and ASCII/non-ASCII should be tested.
* not only the success cases but also the expected failures should be tested.
* an (invalid) file extension may contain non-ASCII characters (but should still lead to a reasonable error message
* If local files, the actual file system encoding is unknown, it may be hard to test `sys.getfilesystemencoding()`.
* as always, `'RAW'` files are special, so separate test cases have to be constructed for them.

try:

issue_title: onResolved handler is called in the same execution context
issue_body:
It seems to me that current implementation is non-compliant with https://promisesaplus.com/ spec with regard to

> 2.2.4 Fulfilled or onRejected must not be called until the execution context stack contains only platform code. [3.1].

This manifests in inconsistencies between situations where Promise is resolved immediately (such us during unit-testing with mock data) vs situations where it resolves asynchronously after a while, leading to some bugs in implementation manifesting only after deploying to production.

Consider following toy program with a subtle bug:

``` python
import concurrent.futures
import time
executor = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=40000);
from promise import Promise

def identity(x):
  #time.sleep(1)
  return x
def promise_something(x):
  return Promise.promisify(executor.submit(identity,x));


print Promise.all([ promise_something(x).then(lambda result: result*x)  for x in [0,1,2,3]]).get()
```

It is supposed to produce sequence of squares, and indeed when run, it prints:

```
$ python concurrency_test.py
[0, 1, 4, 9]
```

But there is a bug lurking in the implementation: the `x` variable used in list comprehension is bound by reference to the closure of `lambda: result*x`, not by value, so the code works only because the lambda passed to `then()` is called synchronously and the current value of `x` is used. If however, you uncomment the `time.sleep(1)` line, you will get a different output:

```
$ python concurrency_test.py
[0, 3, 6, 9]
```

This time, the same value of `x`, namely `3` was used for all invocations of the lambda, because that was the value variable `x` retained after iterating over whole array `[0,1,2,3]`.

This is just a toy example, but the more complicated version of the same bug occurred in real life scenario to me in code like this:

``` python
return Promise.all([
    diarize_single_wave(diarizationEngine,pathOfWave).then(lambda result : judge_single_result(path_to_name(pathOfWave),result))
    for pathOfWave in glob.glob(join("input-data","*.wav"))
  ])
```

and it took me a while to figure out what was wrong, as the problem manifested half the time only (namely when run on a clean working directory, without cached results - in case when I already had some cached results on the disk, the Promise returned immediately masking the bug).

Once again: there is a bug in my program, and I know it. But then there is also the Promieses/A+ spec which clearly states, that the callback should not be called before the current stack completes, which I believe leads to more consistent behavior. The README.md of this project proudly claims "Its fully compatible with the Promises/A+ spec", so I think it is a bug in the implementation of this library.

issue_title: 幾個小提議
issue_body:
您好！用了comiccrawler抓了那麼多圖，飆了那麼多車。十分感謝作者編寫的軟體帶來的便利！故有幾個問題，或者說是提議請指教。

1.有關於文件命名！有時候在同一頁網頁抓圖時，up主有時會把兩個相同文件名的圖片放在同一頁，例如001.png，002.png【黑】，003.png，002.png【白】，004.png，005.png......但其中命名為002的兩個文件並不是同一個文件，由於在同一目錄下，軟體抓圖下來時，會用後面的img_002.png【白】覆蓋掉前面已經抓下的img_002.png【黑】。請問能不能實現在同一目錄下，當抓到同一文件名但卻是不同文件的時候，在後抓的圖命名上加上副標題，即002(1).png。這個類似PC同一目錄下，重複文件就在原文件名後添加副標題的重命名。請問可不可以實現？

2.有關於圖源的身份驗證。有些圖源網站是有地區IP限制的，紳士往往需要掛代理才能抓圖。由於網絡環境較差[代理有時當機重啟]，以及原圖源數量過於多，單文件過大[高清大圖通常上數MB]，會造成抓圖不成功。例如抓個4MB的原圖，突然網絡延遲或斷聯了後重聯，軟體就只抓2.33MB的原圖就跳過抓下一個圖了，造成原本4MB的原圖只有2.33MB的壞檔。2.33MB圖片雖然可以打開，但只顯示一半，下半部是灰的一片。我覺得可以參考hash驗證這種方法，例如從網盤下載某分享文件，up主貼出hash值，在紳士下載完這個文件後，校驗hash值，對比up主貼出的hash，確保文件完整性。類比comiccrawler，可以加入這個機制，抓完一張圖，本地與網源同時對比hash，確保圖源完整性，是就跳到下一張圖，否就自動刪除壞檔重新下載。這樣不用在抓完一堆CG後，還要一張張視察。作者您覺得如何？

3.翻看了G託管的許多討論，其中有說到是否需要在軟體中添加代理，個人覺得沒有必要。在使用代理軟體時，設置全局代理，再啟用comiccrawler時就可以規避IP地區限制了，前提是代理的IP在允許的範圍內。推薦影梭，但需要VPS，具體使用及說明就不多說了，作者可以多了解。

說了那麼多廢話，不好意思呢！謝謝閱讀！望comiccralwer功能更強大，使用更方便！

另，不知為何博客無法留言，只能發到這。謝謝！

issue_title: Support for JDBI style folder
issue_body:
JDBI is one of the saner database access libraries in the Java world. To solve the N+1 project (e.g. user with permissions) it allows the developer to specify a function that can fold rows into a hierarchy.

For example, say we have:

```select u.*, p.user_perm from users u inner join permissions p on (p.user_id) where u.id=X```

and let's say that the result is:

```user_id     email     permission
1             foo@b   CAN_DO_X
1             foo@b   CAN_DO_Y
1             foo@b   CAN_DO_Z
```

A folder would be a function that takes a row at a time, which also is passed in an accumulator (a dict of id -> typed object, in this case id->user).

For each row passed in, it would check the accumulator to see if the base object (user) existed. If not, it would make the base user object from the row and add it to the accumulator. It would then unpack the permission from the row, and add it to the list of permissions in the user object.

The output result of the user_folder would be a list of unique users, which might only be one user in this case, each of whom would have a list of permissions attached to them. 

The beauty of this for you is that it doesn't require many changes, but it gives records support for N+1 queries very efficiently. You could add an option "folder" param to the query function; if supplied, call it once per row, and then return the values from the dict.

issue_title: Exceptions that can't be caught
issue_body:
Every not-200 status code is converted to Exception instance. The only way to determine which problem occurred, has to be done by asserting exception message.

If the http status code is not recognized, the error is silenced and response object becomes boolean equals False.

There should be at least one optimove-package specific Error  class that inherits from builtin Exception e.g. OptimoveError. It should hold error code.

The next step is to create error class for every known exception, that inherits from OptimoveError. e.g. OptimoveBadRequest, OptimoveUnauthorized, etc.
Not recognized error, should holds returned by API message and be raised as OptimoveError.

issue_title: Support Section Lambdas
issue_body:
Currently if a section is a function, the function will not be called.

Example:
```python
template = '{{{postcode}}} {{#first}} {{{city}}} || {{{town}}} || {{{village}}} || {{{state}}} {{/first}}'
```
So i define `first` to be a function that does something to the content of the section

```python
def first(content):
    return "not implemented"

data = {
    "postcode": "1234",
    "city": "Mustache City",
    "state": "Nowhere",
    "first": first,
}

print(chevron.render(template, data))
```

What currently happens: `1234 || || || `
What I intend to happen: `1234 not implemented`

Of course in reality when implementing the `first` function proper, i would like to have: `1234 Mustache City`

This is an example from https://github.com/OpenCageData/address-formatting, they use a yaml file for defining how to format an address for another country which contains mustache templates.

issue_title: Fail on invalid matchers?
issue_body:
`valididate_matchers` silently filters out matchers that do not exist. I'm guessing this is to be compatible with vcr. Please correct me if that assumption is wrong.

Ref: https://github.com/sigmavirus24/betamax/blob/51721667484bf25e581cc1435e7085dc53c1aad4/betamax/options.py#L8

I personally would like to hard-fail if a matcher is not found as in my recent testing night there were a number of times I forgot to register the matcher, and was unaware of why my updates to the matcher weren't working.

It would seem that I could manually set `match_options` on the cassette, resulting in an IndexError if a matcher does not exist. However, that doesn't seem like the accepted way to do things.

How would you feel about adding an option, or making it the default, to hard fail when a matcher doesn't exist? I think making the default to fail is good for users, but I understand if that's not desired due to incompatibility with VCR.

issue_title: Horizontal Privilege scalation - Download certificates without privs
issue_body:
Hi,
If you create staff users on Django. Even if they don't have enough privileges to access to the CAs, it's possible to download the CA Certs in PEM format, for example through this URL:

* /admin/django_ca/certificateauthority/1/download/?format=PEM

**Steps to replicate**
1. Create a django project and add django-ca.
2. Add django.admin and synchronize the database.
2. Create the initial CA and superuser
3. Login onto the admin portal as admin
4. Create a user and set as "staff"
5. Login with this user. It won't have enough privs to access to anything by default. But...
6. Open the following URL:
 * /admin/django_ca/certificateauthority/1/download/?format=PEM
The Certificate of the CA will be downloaded.

issue_title: Misunderstanding of the difference between JWS and JWT
issue_body:
Internally Flask-JWT are using the TimedJSONWebSignatureSerializer from its dangerous. Unfortunately this ISN'T a JWT generator. Instead it creates a JWS with some extra information in the header like 'exp'.

According to the JWT specification, all the claims are supposed to be part of the payload NOT the header. (They are allowed to be replicated in the header, but not if omitted in the payload)

This seems to me as quite a big misunderstanding of the specification and should be rectified

issue_title: Class instance encoder ignores type if class was derived from dict or list
issue_body:
Currently, `encoders.class_instance_encode()` contains the following lines:

```python
	if isinstance(obj, list) or isinstance(obj, dict):
		return obj
```

However, this makes it impossible to successfully serialize any class instance that has inherited from either `list` or `dict`:
```python
import json_tricks

class Foo(dict):
    def __init__(self):
        self.x = 2

if  __name__ == '__main__':
    f = Foo()
    print(json_tricks.dumps(f))
```
Output:
```
{}
```
The attribute was entirely lost, and so was the class name.

issue_title: Rendezvous server doesn't close websockets cleanly
issue_body:
It should send a websockets close handshake with a status code, as per:  https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6455#section-7.1.2 

The RFC seems to imply that it's optional to do so, but I'd welcome the change, as other websocket libraries assume a connection that TCP closes without the handshake has closed uncleanly, and raise awkward exceptions.

issue_title: Type annotation required?
issue_body:
For:

    @dataclass
    class C:
        x: int = 0
        y = 0

Is y a field? What are the params to __init__()? Just x? Or x and y?

dataclass doesn't need to look at annotations, so there's no technical reason they'd be required.

My personal preference is to require them. That is, drive field discovery from __annotations__, not something like `[name for name in C.__dict__ if not name.startswith('_')]`

issue_title: preserve precision of input values when generating results
issue_body:
A density entered as 2.2e-4 is reported as 0.00.  Instead, values should be treated as if there is an uncertainty of 1 in the last digit and reported with that uncertainty.

This is primarily an issue with the online calculator from the scattering/activation repo, but the periodictable package may need to be updated to support uncertain values in its calculations to ensure that the errors are propagated correctly.

Integer values (e.g., density = 2 g/cm^3) should perhaps be treated as having unlimited precision, and so the number of digits is driven by the uncertainties in cross sections and half-lives.  That does mean that "2" and "2." will show different results, which could be confusing.

issue_title: Django 2.0 set to require on_delete for related fields
issue_body:
If you try to run the server using the `-Wd` flag, you become plagued with lots of warnings for machina stating that on_delete is set to be required.

     python -Wd manage.py runserver

For example,

>lib\site-packages\machina\apps\forum\abstract_models.py:33: RemovedInDjango20Warning: on_delete will be a required arg for TreeForeignKey in Django 2.0. Set it to models.CASCADE on models and in existing migrations if you want to maintain the current default behavior. See https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.9/ref/models/fields/#django.db.models.ForeignKey.on_delete
  'self', null=True, blank=True, related_name='children', verbose_name=_('Parent'))

I've made a simple regex that you should be able to go through and find and replace all (or at least most) foreign keys that don't currently contain an on_delete to contain this so theres not much effort involved in fixing.

    (ForeignKey|OneToOneField)\(((?:(?!on_delete|ForeignKey|OneToOneField)[^\)])*)\)

replace with

    $1($2, on_delete=models.CASCADE)

See [my stackoverflow question](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/41571281/easy-way-to-set-on-delete-across-entire-application) for more info on what this regex does if required (or ask me! :) ).

Many thanks!

issue_title: `toc` Extra raises `TypeError` in Python3
issue_body:
When `python-markdown2.py` is run with Python3, with the experimental `toc` extra enabled, a `TypeError` is raised:

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./python-markdown2/lib/markdown2.py", line 2458, in <module>
    sys.exit( main(sys.argv) )
  File "./python-markdown2/lib/markdown2.py", line 2443, in main
    html.toc_html.encode(sys.stdout.encoding or "utf-8", 'xmlcharrefreplace'))
TypeError: Can't convert 'bytes' object to str implicitly
```

Despite the exception, the HTML code is generated and is identical to that created with Python2.

### Reproduce ###

```bash
$ python2 -c 'import sys; print(sys.version_info)'
sys.version_info(major=2, minor=7, micro=10, releaselevel='final', serial=0)

$ python2 ./python-markdown2/lib/markdown2.py --extras toc index.text > index-python2.html

$ python3 -c 'import sys; print(sys.version_info)'
sys.version_info(major=3, minor=4, micro=3, releaselevel='final', serial=0)

$ python3 ./python-markdown2/lib/markdown2.py --extras toc index.text > index-python3.html
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./python-markdown2/lib/markdown2.py", line 2458, in <module>
    sys.exit( main(sys.argv) )
  File "./python-markdown2/lib/markdown2.py", line 2443, in main
    html.toc_html.encode(sys.stdout.encoding or "utf-8", 'xmlcharrefreplace'))

issue_title: Runaway loop, never returns
issue_body:
I have an Exchange server with misconfigured autodiscover (doesn't return a good response), and your autodiscover never returns, it just runs forever (see more below)... I have been writing Exchange code for Python for a couple of years, and I'd be happy to join your project and then I could fix issues like this.

I found the issue in your code:
_**util.py line 253**_

You are using a "while True:" loop, which is dangerous in Python, I had some similar issues with my project. I recommend you put in some type of limit like:

```
max_loops = 10
i = 0
while i < max_loops:
    i += 1
    # do stuff
```

issue_title: [Documentation] Times of emails
issue_body:
E-Mails seem to have four time fields:

* 'datetime_created',
* 'datetime_sent',
* 'datetime_received',
* 'last_modified_time'

Are there any guarantees about the content of those fields? What can the sender manipulate?

Lets have a normal scenario: I use Thunderbird. I make a draft on `2017-02-20 22:15` and send it on `2017-02-21 09:30` (my mail servers time - but my local clock is by one minute, so `2017-02-21 09:29`). It arrives on the recipients mail server at `2017-02-21 09:31` and he marks the email as read at `2017-02-21 11:45`.

Questions:

* Is "datetime_created" now `2017-02-20 22:15` or `2017-02-21 09:30` or `2017-02-21 09:29` (on usual mail clients)?
* Is "last_modified_time" now `2017-02-21 09:31` or `2017-02-21 11:45`?
* Is "datetime_sent" the time on senders machine or the time on the senders mail server?

issue_title: Check for statistical significance of changes
issue_body:
I think it would be nice to not only know if the mean and other related stats changed between one version and another but to know if that difference is statistically significant. Due to the highly skewed nature of most timing data, a standard T-Test would be invalid, so I think a [Mann-Whitney U Test](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mann–Whitney_U_test) would be appropriate. I just found this project, and I'm still looking through the code base to figure out where I could implement the test, but I would need access to all the timing data from the previous run. Some pointers on where to start would be helpful.

The Mann Whitney U Test is implemented in [scipy](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy/reference/generated/scipy.stats.mannwhitneyu.html), but that would be a non-trivial dependency to add to the project. It's not particularly difficult to implement, but writing our own would, of course, be more code to maintain.

issue_title: Added Continuous Integration through Travis CI
issue_body:
Testing/Continuous Integration has been added through Travis CI. It has taken a lot of commits to hammer out some issues preventing it from working well. There is still a bit more. More importantly, I found a syntax regression in the master branch that causes compatibility for Python 3.0 to 3.2 to be lost. That should be pretty easy to fix. In addition, I have been stumbling upon a few unknown dependencies of the package. Namely, full functionality requires that libhdf5 >= 1.8.7 and Numpy can't be too old (I am still working on pinning the exact version down). There does seem to be another one with respect to unicode conversions (Python 2.6 and 2.7). Either that or there is a bug. I am going to be trying to track these down and fix the code accordingly and add the discovered dependencies (or different versions) to the documentation. Expect individual issues for these in the near future.

In many ways, the very old software that the Travis CI virtual machines is running has been a good thing - it has helped uncover unknown dependencies.

The testing can be seen at

https://travis-ci.org/frejanordsiek/hdf5storage

issue_title: Compile error when broadcasting
issue_body:
I'm running into an issue when broadcasting inside a pythran function a minimal example is below:

```python
import numpy as np

#pythran export  testfail(complex128 [], float [][], complex128 [], int)
def testfail(E, testangles, symbols, N):
     comp_angles = np.exp(1.j * testangles)
     Eangles = E[:, np.newaxis] * comp_angles
     dd = Eangles[:,:,np.newaxis] - symbols
     return dd
```

this results in a compile error with both gcc and clang. 
[gcc.log](https://github.com/serge-sans-paille/pythran/files/1998960/gcc.log)
[clang.log](https://github.com/serge-sans-paille/pythran/files/1998961/clang.log)

I can work around the error by putting the second newaxis subtraction into an explicit loop, however this is very slow  compared to unwrapping all loops. Example code below:
```python
import numpy as np

#pythran export  testworks(complex128 [], float [][], complex128 [], int)
def testworks(E, testangles, symbols, N):
     comp_angles = np.exp(1.j * testangles)
     Eangles = E[:, np.newaxis] * comp_angles
     dd = np.zeros((Eangles.shape[0], Eangles.shape[1], symbols.shape[0]), dtype=E.dtype)
     for i in range(symbols.shape[0]):
         dd[:,:,i] = Eangles - symbols[i]
     return dd

#pythran export  testfaster(complex128 [], float [][], complex128 [], int)
def testfaster(E, testangles, symbols, N):
     comp_angles = np.exp(1.j * testangles)
     Eangles = E[:, np.newaxis] * comp_angles
     dd = np.zeros((Eangles.shape[0], Eangles.shape[1], symbols.shape[0]), dtype=E.dtype)
     for j in range(Eangles.shape[0]):
         for k in range(Eangles.shape[1]):
            for i in range(symbols.shape[0]):
                dd[j,k,i] = Eangles[j,k] - symbols[i]
     return dd
```

```python
In [1]: import ptest2

In [2]: s=np.random.rand(10**4)+1.j*np.random.rand(10**4)

In [3]: s2=np.random.rand(40)+1.j*np.random.rand(40)

In [4]: a=np.linspace(0, np.pi/2, 50).reshape(1,-1)

In [5]: timeit ptest2.testworks(s, a, s2, 10)
407 ms ± 3.34 ms per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 1 loop each)

In [6]: timeit ptest2.testfaster(s, a, s2, 10)
134 ms ± 864 µs per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 10 loops each)

In [7]: 
```
this seems to scale with the size of s2 and the difference also becomes larger when enabling compiler flags (i did not use any here).

issue_title: Delta and full update JSON on CRUDEvent
issue_body:
My team is considering having a delta field of the changes, as opposed to the full JSON object on `post_save`.

I haven't started my implementation yet, but I was curious what you thought of the following extension:

- In the signal code, let the output of the callback/hook code (as we know, it's optional), return False, or True, or some sort of enum-like value. In this case it could be FULL, DELTA. That could be a little confusing, but maybe not so bad. Or, have it be a different kind of hook -- different from the one currently implemented in the code.

- Allow saving both the FULL and DELTA on the CRUDEvent, so the project can determine if they want both or one or the other, always, or programmatically. This is in the same vein as above. It unfortunately would potentially duplicate the JSON in the worst case, if both are used on a given object operation.

I want to keep it as simple as possible, but still flexible for the consumer's needs. Are you interested in such a change?

issue_title: Generating binary data causes an error
issue_body:
Greetings,

I have noticed that if my view returns a binary document (a PDF document in my case), I receive the following error:

    [29/Mar/2015 20:10:13] "POST /wallet/pdf_backup_key/ HTTP/1.1" 200 0
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/wsgiref/handlers.py", line 138, in run
        self.finish_response()
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/wsgiref/handlers.py", line 180, in finish_response
        self.write(data)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/wsgiref/handlers.py", line 279, in write
        self._write(data)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/wsgiref/handlers.py", line 453, in _write
        self.stdout.write(data)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/socket.py", line 391, in write
        return self._sock.send(b)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/ssl.py", line 679, in send
        v = self._sslobj.write(data)
    ssl.SSLEOFError: EOF occurred in violation of protocol (_ssl.c:1636)

My code is the following:
    response = HttpResponse(content_type='application/pdf')
    response['Content-Disposition'] = 'attachment; filename="backup_key.pdf"'

What is noticeable is that in normal http mode it works perfectly.

I have discovered that the class `HttpResponse`, by default, creates a content which is `b''`, which does not seem to be allowed by ssl. It is not important if, later, I add further data (my pdf document).
I have temporarily solved the problem by specifying a content, such as `response = HttpResponse(content='hello', ...)`, but it is a silly solution.

issue_title: django.core.urlresolvers.NoReverseMatch: Reverse for 'password_reset_confirm'
issue_body:
**Django:** 1.8
**Python:** 3.4

When a user tries to reset password, it throws exception:

```
django.core.urlresolvers.NoReverseMatch: Reverse for 'password_reset_confirm' with
    arguments '()' and keyword arguments '{'token': '444-9887c242917d1b6b7368',
    'uidb64': b'Mg'}' not found. 0 pattern(s) tried: []
```

[This thread](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/19985103/django-1-6-and-django-registration-built-in-authentication-views-not-picked-up) on Stackoverflow solved it for me.

>The reason for this error is that the _django.contrib.auth.views_ use different url names than the _registration.auth_urls_ ones. To patch this problem, override the default urls until _django-registration-redux_ gets updated for django 1.6, and use the same names as Django.

```python
from django.contrib.auth import views as auth_views

urlpatterns = patterns('',

      #override the default urls
      url(r'^password/change/$',
                    auth_views.password_change,
                    name='password_change'),
      url(r'^password/change/done/$',
                    auth_views.password_change_done,
                    name='password_change_done'),
      url(r'^password/reset/$',
                    auth_views.password_reset,
                    name='password_reset'),
      url(r'^password/reset/done/$',
                    auth_views.password_reset_done,
                    name='password_reset_done'),
      url(r'^password/reset/complete/$',
                    auth_views.password_reset_complete,
                    name='password_reset_complete'),
      url(r'^password/reset/confirm/(?P<uidb64>[0-9A-Za-z]+)-(?P<token>.+)/$',
                    auth_views.password_reset_confirm,
                    name='password_reset_confirm'),

      #and now add the registration urls
      url(r'^accounts/$', include('registration.backends.default.urls')),
)
```

TranslationQueryset.get_or_create fails to create
issue_body:
The get_or_create method is not overridden in TranslationQueryset; as a result, calling code like

    Model.objects.language('en').get_or_create(**creation_args)

will fail, as the TranslationQueryset will defer to django's get_or_create, which will naively attempt to create a new TranslationModel with the creation args which were intended for the Translatable Model.

I have attached a set of test cases in a pull request (https://github.com/KristianOellegaard/django-hvad/pull/19).

issue_title: tree_path TextField stored as NCLOB in Oracle
issue_body:
Oracle unfortunately stores TextFields as NCLOBs, preventing them from being indexed and used in DISTINCT queries. Django documentation discussing this is here:

http://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.2/ref/databases/#textfield-limitations

This prevents threadedcomments from working correctly in Oracle. Trying to post comments to the database results in the error "ORA-00932: inconsistent datatypes: expected - got NCLOB".

issue_title: support additionalProperties false
IBAN comparison with other apps is not correct
issue_body:
Hi, Great work on this module. I'm probably going to use the VAT field in my project.

I'm the author of django-iban which was the basis for the django-localflavor IBAN and BIC fields. Your comparison documentation is not correct and it would be nice to have this corrected.
```
This module also supports a IBANField, but does not validate against regular expressions, rather validating the length of the IBAN for each country code. The validation provided by :mod:`internationalflavor` is more extensive by using regular expressions.
```
The IBAN fields in django-localflavor does in fact check the length for the specific country but it also does a full checksum validation as per the official IBAN validation guide (just like your code).

https://github.com/django/django-localflavor/blob/1.1/localflavor/generic/validators.py#L111

As far as I can see, there is no real functional difference between your version and the version in django-localflavor. But I would love to work together on making the django-localflavor version better if there is a problem with it. The reason I contributed the code to django-localflavor was so that there could be a community maintained version of this validation rather than a bunch of solo devs trying to hack this out on our own.

```
Additionally, we provide a :class:`BICField` for SWIFT BIC validation.
```
This is also not correct. django-localflavor has a BICField as well.

https://github.com/django/django-localflavor/blob/1.1/localflavor/generic/models.py#L55

Like I said, if there is a problem with the localflavor versions of the IBAN or BIC fields, I would be very happy to see a bug report or review a PR against django-localflavor. At the very least, your documentation should be updated to accurately reflect the differences between the two versions (which seems to be nothing).

issue_title: Make use of ._response_object?
issue_body:
Hi Box team,

On the main README under "Metadata", you recommend retrieving file metadata with:
 ```client.file(file_id='SOME_FILE_ID').metadata().get()```. However, after playing with the SDK and reading some of the source code, it appears that this line is making two API calls and only works for File objects. Why is this preferred over simply taking advantage of an object's ```._response_object``` attribute, which is made available for any BaseObject after a single API request?

issue_title: `boxsdk.exception.BoxAPIException` Documentation Error
issue_body:
In [boxsdk/exception.py](../blob/master/boxsdk/exception.py) (line 115 in `master`, but same problem in `1.5`), the documentation of `boxsdk.exception.BoxAPIException` says the return type of the `.code` property is `int`, but I believe it is actually `unicode`. Presumably a copy/paste from the immediately-above `.status` property, whose return type really is `int`.

Additionally, the type of the `code` parameter to the `__init__` of the same class is documented as `unicode` earlier in the file.

I have found the [attrs](http://www.attrs.org/en/stable/) library useful.

issue_title: Question on view and indexes
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a quick conceptual question.

Let us assume I have a database with three fields A,B and C. Now, I have added views using fields A and B. 

Now, I can create an index using the following manner:

``
index1  = my_database.create_query_index(fields=['a'],index_name="index_a")
index2 = my_database.create_query_index(fields=['b'],index_name="index_b")
index3 = my_database.create_query_index(fields=['a','b'],index_name="index_a_b")
```
Now I want to query using a selector from both fields a and b.

```
selector = {'a': {'$lt': 30}, 'b':{'$eq':5}}
docs = my_database.get_query_result(selector,use_index = index_a)

selector ={'a': {'$lt': 30}, 'b':{'$eq':5}}
docs = my_database.get_query_result(selector,use_index = index_a_b)
```

What does it mean to create an index using multiple fields (whose views are added)?
Which one will be faster and why?

issue_title: [R] Is the mxnet always minimize the custom loss function?
issue_body:
Package used (Python/R/Scala/Julia): R
Before I write my own custom loss function with mx.metric.custom, I checked the `https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/blob/master/R-package/R/metric.R`.
I find two functions: mx.metric.accuracy is used as a metric for classification and mx.metric.rmse is used as a metric for regression. 
So far as I know, the model should minimize the loss function like MSE. But minimize accuracy indicates a bad model. When I test the mx.metric.accuracy, the increasing accuracy of each epoch indicates the mxnet know how to treat the loss function.
This confuses me when I write my own loss function. I do not  know when the mxnet will maximum the loss function and when the mxnet will minimize the loss function. Could you explain this stuff? Thank you.

## Minimum reproducible example
```python
from data import mnist_iterator
import mxnet as mx
import numpy as np
import logging

data = mx.symbol.Variable('data')
data = mx.symbol.Cast(data=data, name='cast', dtype=np.float16) # cast to float16
fc1 = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data = data, name='fc1', num_hidden=128)
act1 = mx.symbol.Activation(data = fc1, name='relu1', act_type="relu")
fc2 = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data = act1, name = 'fc2', num_hidden = 64) 
act2 = mx.symbol.Activation(data = fc2, name='relu2', act_type="relu")
fc3 = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data = act2, name='fc3', num_hidden=10)
fc3 = mx.sym.Cast(data=fc3, dtype=np.float32)
mlp = mx.symbol.SoftmaxOutput(data = fc3, name = 'softmax')

# data

train, val = mnist_iterator(batch_size=100, input_shape = (784,))

# train

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

model = mx.model.FeedForward(
    ctx = mx.gpu(), symbol = mlp, num_epoch = 20, 
    learning_rate = 0.1, momentum = 0.9, wd = 0.00001)

def norm_stat(d):
    return mx.nd.norm(d)/np.sqrt(d.size)
mon = mx.mon.Monitor(100, norm_stat)
model.fit(X=train, eval_data=val, monitor=mon,
          batch_end_callback = mx.callback.Speedometer(100, 100))
```
## What have you tried to solve it?
Error comes from ```mx.nd.norm``` which doesn't support float16.
A quick (dirty) fix is to replace 
```python
def norm_stat(d):
    return mx.nd.norm(d)/np.sqrt(d.size)
```

by

```python
def norm_stat(d):
    return mx.nd.norm(d.astype(np.float32))/np.sqrt(d.size)
```

Any better solution?

issue_title: how to limit threads number in windows
issue_body:
When training with cpu mode, openmp will create multi threads.
but toward some very small net model, the performance of using multi threads is not better or even worse.
In linux, by set the enviorment  variable OMP_NUM_THREADS = 1,
the threads number can be limited. but in windows, it doesn't work.
I have tried some other enviorment varable:
OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS
MXNET_CPU_WORKER_NTHREADS
MXNET_CPU_PRIORITY_NTHREADS
but the result is same.
dose any one have some suggestion?

issue_title: How to handle video data with an existing image classification network
issue_body:
I am trying to build a network that can process several video frames and output a class label with a softmax function. The baseline is like this: Frames from the same video are first processed by a normal CNN(i.e. inception-bn). Then, they are fused by a temporal pooling layer which generates a single feature representation for a video. A soft-max can be added after that to predict the label I need.
The problem is the data inputs of those existing imagenet pre-trained model(saved as json files) are already fixed. If I want to feed 10 video files(batch size), 15 frames per video, to the network each time. There are usually two different solutions:
a) The shapes of the input data and label are set as (10, 15, 3, height, width) and (10,) respectively. Then I need to reshape the data to (150, 3, height, width) and extract feature for each frame with the inception-bn symbol. Before the temporal pooling layer, a reverse reshape layer is also added to transform the features to (10, 15, feat-dim) again. But I do not know how to replace the original data symbol with a data->reshape symbol.
b) The shapes of the input data and label are set as (150, 3, height, width) and (10, ) respectively. Thus, each frame can be processed by the inception-bn separately. But there is another problem, since the first dims of data shape and label shape are different, neither batch-size=150 nor batch-size=15 can really work during the training phase(the data parallel mechanism would slice the data and label based on the data batch-size).

Can any one come and provide some help about it?

issue_title: Optional model imports collision resolver.
issue_body:
`shell_plus` imports all models on startup. 
But if two different models has exactly the same name, 
only one of them(this which was imported last) is accessible.
Both models should be accessible, one of them should be imported with alias.

For example suppose that we have two apps:
- languages with model Language (for example English)
- programming with model Language (for example Python)

I suggest to add
`MODEL_IMPORTS_COLLISION_RESOLVER` option to django settings, 
which is path to python function. 
This function will have one argument: `Dict[str, List[str]]`.

Key will be model name, value will be list of modules, which has model with this name.
Function will return `Dict[str, List[Union[str, Tuple[str, str]]]`, where key is module name, 
and value list of model names or tuples (model_name, alias). 
We use currently this structure to collect all model imports.

Two functions to resolve conflicts should be added:

- legacy function, which behaves exactly as now.
- app_name function which returns all imports from legacy version and aditionally aliased 
imports for models with duplicate name. It can also for performance reasons remove imports, 
which will be overwritten. 
It will concat app_name with model_name.

In previously defined example this function will receive:
```
{
	'Language': ['languages.models', 'programming.models'],
} + django models
```

and will return 
```
{
	'languages.models' [('Language', 'languages_Language')], 
	# import without alias from languages is not needed because it will be overwritten 
	'programming.models': ['Language', 'programming_Language']
	# import without alias from programming is needed, becase it won't be overwritten 
} + django models
```

We should also allow user to pass his own function, 
and describe in documentation what it should receive and return.

Is this feature is acceptable?
I want to implement this feature and submit PR if it is.

Guys,
For the second time [1] , jQuery Migrate v 1.2.1 reverts back any jQuery to behave like version < 1.9.0
In fact as described on https://code.google.com/p/domxsswiki/wiki/jQuery it's quite known that jQuery was patched to mitigate several XSS potential exposition [2] since version 1.6.1.

At the moment, it seems that jQueryMigrate does not follow the behavios of the different versions of jQuery, and it tries to fix only the jQuery version 1.6.1 pattern /^#.*/.
We would like to have a behavior that is at least expected to the version jQueryMigrate is trying to "migrate"/patch.

Ps. If you ask for a PoC I'll send it privately.

[1] http://blog.mindedsecurity.com/2013/04/jquery-migrate-is-sink-too.html
[2] http://bugs.jquery.com/ticket/9521

issue_title: Dashes in .data() names behavior change
issue_body:
We're using camelCase for data names where possible, and only use the dash-name if it exists in the object already. Ideally, API callers should use camelCase for accessing data names rather than dashes. If the caller gets the raw data object they'll see camelCase if they put in dash-name via the API. issue_title: Some messages lost when copy is True

I have defined a utility function to handle one-time messages which creates a new PUB socket and publishes a message, something like this:

```python
@classmethod
def broadcast(cls, ctx, message):
    socket = ctx.socket(zmq.PUB)
    socket.send_multipart([b'broadcast', message])
```

```
socket.send_multipart([b'broadcast', message], copy=False)

issue_title: Wheels without bundled ZeroMQ
issue_body:
I apology in advance if question is lame as I'm not familiar with manylinux and cython to the bone.

Currently pyzmq wheels for Linux are shipped with pre-built zeromq. libzmq is available for the popular distributions either from the mainstream or from custom repositories. To my understanding the convenience of manylinux is to ship only essential binaries and allow the linker to resolve dependencies. That allows system administrator to independently update libraries.

In my case pyzmq, even 17.0.0b1, is still shipped with libzmq 4.1.6 while I'd like to use 4.2.2 (no draft) due to important bug fixes. My only options are to either build my own wheels or install everything required for a manual build right where I deploy.

issue_title: Minimize session.run calls

issue_title: Bounded Integer
issue_body:
The `Int` traitlet supports bounds, but not the `Integer` traitlet:

```python
import traitlets

class A(traitlets.HasTraits):
    bounded_integer = traitlets.Integer(default_value=traitlets.Undefined, min=5, max=100)

class B(traitlets.HasTraits):
    bounded_int = traitlets.Int(default_value=traitlets.Undefined, min=5, max=100)

a = A(bounded_integer=200) # raises a TraitError, as expected
a = B(bounded_int=200) # does not raise
```

This behaviour is a little confusing, since the docs recommend using the Integer traitlet rather than Int.

Is there any reason this was implemented this way (see PR #33)?

I'm happy to put in a PR for a bounded Integer if this would be useful.

issue_title: Dynamic Luigi task
issue_body:
I am building a wrapper for Luigi Tasks and I ran into a snag with the [`Register`][1] class that's actually an ABC metaclass and not being pickable when I create a dynamic `type`.  I'm looking for help (or maybe ideas) in resolving.

The following code, more or less, is what I'm using to develop the dynamic class.

<!-- language: lang-py -->

    class TaskWrapper(object):
        '''Luigi Spark Factory from the provided JobClass

        Args:
            JobClass(ScrubbedClass): The job to wrap
            options: Options as passed into the JobClass
        '''

        def __new__(self, JobClass, **options):
            # Validate we have a good job
            valid_classes = (
                ScrubbedClass01,
                # ScrubbedClass02,
                # ScrubbedClass03,
            )
            if any(vc == JobClass for vc in valid_classes) or not issubclass(JobClass, valid_classes):
                raise TypeError('Job is not the correct class: {}'.format(JobClass))

            # Build a luigi task class dynamically
            luigi_identifier = 'Task'
            job_name = JobClass.__name__
            job_name = job_name.replace('Pail', '')
            if not job_name.endswith(luigi_identifier):
                job_name += luigi_identifier

            LuigiTask = type(job_name, (PySparkTask, ), {})

            for k, v in options.items():
                setattr(LuigiTask, k, luigi.Parameter())

            def main(self, sc, *args):
                job = JobClass(**options)
                return job._run()

            LuigiTask.main = main

            return LuigiTask


When I run my calling function, however, I get `PicklingError: Can't pickle <class 'abc.ScrubbedNameTask'>: attribute lookup abc.ScrubbedNameTask failed`.


Calling Function:

<!-- language: lang-py -->

    def create_task(JobClass, **options):
        LuigiTask = TaskWrapper(JobClass, **options)
        # Add parameters
        parameters = {
            d: options.get(d)
            for d in dir(LuigiTask)
            if not d.startswith('_')
            if isinstance(getattr(LuigiTask, d), luigi.Parameter)
            if d in options
        }

        task = LuigiTask(**parameters)
        return task


  [1]: http://luigi.readthedocs.io/en/stable/_modules/luigi/task_register.html#Register

Also posted on [Stack Overflow](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/39217180/how-to-dynamically-create-a-luigi-task).

issue_title: S3 multipart directory copy not working
issue_body:
The method call [here](https://github.com/spotify/luigi/blame/master/luigi/s3.py#L361) is using an incorrect method signature which means when trying to copy a folder of files with files larger than 64mb, the copy will fail. Unfortunately because of the `moto` bug that prevents the s3 multipart_copy test from working, this isn't covered in the test suite.

Also, we have two sets of threading methodologies here, where the `multipart_copy` doesn't use the same thread pool as the the copy method.

I'll try and consolidate both of these methods into one `copy` method that share a common thread pool.

issue_title: Don't consider params that don't subclass SuperType as request parameters
Integrate with the mypy daemon
issue_body:
@gvanrossum creating a separate issue to discuss this.

I've poked around mypy a little and I'm very pleased at the sight of the fscache module. The reason pyls doesn't integrate with pylint is because we have no way of telling pylint to read from our workspace (in-memory view of what's in the client's buffers).

I imagine it would be pretty easy to patch the FileSystemCache class to read from our workspace. The downsides of this approach are:

* FileSystemCache isn't public API so it could change at any time. 
* We can't easily patch something inside the mypy daemon. And even if we can, we need to make it talk back to pyls.

Another approach might be to run the mypy daemon within the pyls process. The daemon's lifetime is then tied to that of your client which seems quite reasonable, particularly if we make use of the remote caching feature. I might give this a go.

issue_title: Yet another segmentation fault of h5py
issue_body: When running really large classical computations (i.e. SHARE) sometimes the `.save_hcurves` method fails with a segfault. This never happens in regular computations, nor in the tests and in the demos. The reason probably is that such method is writing on an extensible dataset which is very large, thus exciting a bug in h5py (out of memory in the heap??). Even if the computation runs (since the bug is random) it is still slow. Probably we should not use an extensible dataset and go back to regular fixed-size datasets, which are safer and faster. The price to pay is that we will write some zero curves, but it is a small price.

I am using celery on Windows. And I set CELERYD_TASK_TIME_LIMIT to 1200. Depends on the document,  'The worker processing the task will be killed and replaced with a new one when this is exceeded.'

But when the executing time of my task exceeds the CELERYD_TASK_TIME_LIMIT setting, I got the following error:

> [2015-01-04 12:56:27,994: ERROR/MainProcess] Thread 'TimeoutHandler' crashed: AttributeError("'module' object has no attribute 'SIGKILL'",)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\billiard\pool.py", line 472, in run
    return self.body()
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\billiard\pool.py", line 708, in body
    for _ in self.handle_timeouts():
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\billiard\pool.py", line 699, in handle_timeouts
    on_hard_timeout(job)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\billiard\pool.py", line 653, in on_hard_timeout
    self._trywaitkill(process)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\billiard\pool.py", line 666, in _trywaitkill
    _kill(worker.pid, signal.SIGKILL)
AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'SIGKILL'

issue_title: Changing crontab of one task makes all tasks with the same old crontab execute
issue_body: Say I have 3 tasks with "0 1 * * *". If I pick one and change is to "10 * * * *", then at that time (say 15:10 if I changed it after 14:10), all 3 tasks will get executed, instead of just the one I changed. This is with version 1.0.1
